results.label: Dyspnea / heart failure or volume overload
results.context:
General medicine
Adult
validated clinical intent workup
intent: Dyspnea / heart failure or volume overload
intent_id: dyspnea_hf_v1
intent tags: dyspnea; hypoxia; heart_failure; acute_heart_failure; volume_overload; cardiopulmonary; pulmonary_exam; perfusion; vitals; vital_signs; edema; bnp; nt_probnp; cxr; ecg; troponin; renal_function; electrolytes; red_flag
clinical bundles: dyspnea_hf
required domains: vitals; lungs/work of breathing; JVP/edema; heart sounds/perfusion; Dyspnea/HF initial tests; respiratory/hemodynamic red flags
avoid labels: Murphy sign; CVA tenderness; Vibration sense

safety.label: Blood pressure
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: hypotension, hypertension, shock physiology, pain response, medication effect, or disposition-changing instability.
safety.management_change: Abnormal blood pressure can change acuity, fluids, antihypertensive decisions, endocrine-crisis treatment, monitoring, and escalation.

safety.label: Heart rate
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: tachycardia, bradycardia, irregular rhythm, shock physiology, adrenergic state, infection, pain, or medication effect.
safety.management_change: Abnormal heart rate can change ECG/telemetry urgency, volume or endocrine-crisis assessment, infection severity, medication review, and escalation.

safety.label: Respiratory rate
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: respiratory distress, compensation, shock physiology, acid-base stress, sedation, or cardiopulmonary deterioration.
safety.management_change: Abnormal respiratory rate or labored breathing can change oxygen/ventilation assessment, acid-base urgency, monitoring level, and escalation.

safety.label: Oxygen saturation / support
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: hypoxemia, escalating oxygen need, respiratory failure risk, shock physiology, pulmonary disease, or cardiopulmonary deterioration.
safety.management_change: Abnormal oxygen saturation or rising oxygen requirement can change respiratory support, imaging urgency, pulmonary/infectious/cardiac framing, monitoring level, and escalation.

safety.label: Temperature
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: fever, hypothermia, infection, drug reaction, endocrine crisis, inflammatory disease, or systemic illness.
safety.management_change: Fever or hypothermia can change infection/endocrine-crisis workup, isolation, medication decisions, monitoring, and escalation.

history.label: What changed with breathing: onset, exertional dyspnea, orthopnea or PND, cough/wheeze, chest pain, leg swelling, weight change, oxygen need, or missed diuretics?
history.reason: Dyspnea/heart-failure source and severity: pulmonary congestion, obstructive/infectious symptoms, ischemic symptoms, oxygen escalation, or volume change.
history.management_change: The answer changes oxygen/support strategy, diuresis versus alternate pulmonary workup, ECG/troponin/imaging urgency, medication review, and disposition.

history.label: Is breathing worse at rest, with exertion, lying flat, during sleep, or with chest pain, cough, wheeze, fever, leg swelling, or higher oxygen need?
history.reason: Respiratory severity: tachypnea, labored breathing, accessory muscle use, inability to speak, fatigue, cyanosis, or impending respiratory failure.
history.management_change: Increased work of breathing changes oxygen/ventilatory support, need for urgent reassessment, ED/ICU escalation, and timing of CXR/ECG/labs.
history.evidence: AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; ATS_CAP_2025; IDSA_CAP_PATHWAY_2019; SM25; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX

core.label: Posterior lung sounds
core.reason: Differentiates congestion, wheeze, consolidation, effusion, or other pulmonary causes.
core.diagnostic_target: Lung findings: crackles, wheeze, rhonchi, diminished sounds, asymmetry, or focal ventilation abnormality.
core.management_change: New or changed lung findings can change diuresis, bronchodilator, antibiotic, imaging, oxygen/support, or escalation decisions.

core.label: JVP
core.reason: Checks congestion, cardiac auscultation, and perfusion relevant to fluid and diuretic strategy.
core.diagnostic_target: Heart-failure/volume clue: elevated JVP, edema, S3/gallop or murmur, irregular rhythm, or perfusion deficit.
core.management_change: Congestion or poor perfusion findings can change diuretic/fluid strategy, telemetry/ECG/echo urgency, respiratory support, and disposition.
core.evidence: Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension

core.label: Lower extremity edema
core.reason: Checks congestion, cardiac auscultation, and perfusion relevant to fluid and diuretic strategy.
core.diagnostic_target: Heart-failure/volume clue: elevated JVP, edema, S3/gallop or murmur, irregular rhythm, or perfusion deficit.
core.management_change: Congestion or poor perfusion findings can change diuretic/fluid strategy, telemetry/ECG/echo urgency, respiratory support, and disposition.
core.evidence: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources

core.label: Heart sounds
core.reason: Checks congestion, cardiac auscultation, and perfusion relevant to fluid and diuretic strategy.
core.diagnostic_target: Heart-failure/volume clue: elevated JVP, edema, S3/gallop or murmur, irregular rhythm, or perfusion deficit.
core.management_change: Congestion or poor perfusion findings can change diuretic/fluid strategy, telemetry/ECG/echo urgency, respiratory support, and disposition.
core.evidence: JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds

core.label: Radial pulses
core.reason: Checks congestion, cardiac auscultation, and perfusion relevant to fluid and diuretic strategy.
core.diagnostic_target: Heart-failure/volume clue: elevated JVP, edema, S3/gallop or murmur, irregular rhythm, or perfusion deficit.
core.management_change: Congestion or poor perfusion findings can change diuretic/fluid strategy, telemetry/ECG/echo urgency, respiratory support, and disposition.
core.evidence: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources

core.label: Posterior thorax inspection
core.reason: Assesses respiratory effort and distress before deciding whether bedside support or escalation is needed.
core.diagnostic_target: Work of breathing: accessory muscle use, retractions, asymmetry, fatigue, or distress.
core.management_change: Increased work of breathing can change oxygen/ventilatory support, monitoring location, and urgency of reassessment.

core.label: Work of breathing observation
core.reason: Dyspnea workups must start with respiratory effort because distress changes oxygen, ventilatory support, monitoring, and disposition before the etiology is fully proven.
core.diagnostic_target: Respiratory severity: tachypnea, labored breathing, accessory muscle use, inability to speak, fatigue, cyanosis, or impending respiratory failure.
core.management_change: Increased work of breathing changes oxygen/ventilatory support, need for urgent reassessment, ED/ICU escalation, and timing of CXR/ECG/labs.
core.evidence: AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; ATS_CAP_2025; IDSA_CAP_PATHWAY_2019; SM25; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX

conditional.label: PMI
conditional.reason: Adds apical impulse only when dyspnea overlaps with heart failure, cardiomyopathy, pulmonary hypertension, or volume-overload context.
conditional.diagnostic_target: Precordial structural clue: displaced/diffuse apical impulse or visible heave in cardiomyopathy/volume-overload context.
conditional.management_change: A displaced or diffuse impulse can support cardiac structural framing and echo/cardiology review when consistent with the rest of the case.
conditional.evidence: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac exam resources

tests.label: Dyspnea/HF initial tests and reference thresholds
tests.reason: Dyspnea with possible HF or volume overload needs objective cardiopulmonary testing because exam findings alone cannot reliably distinguish HF, pneumonia, COPD/asthma, PE, ischemia, renal failure, or anemia.
tests.diagnostic_target: HF/dyspnea etiology and safety screen: congestion/cardiomegaly or alternative disease on CXR, rhythm/ischemia on ECG, natriuretic peptide support or exclusion of HF, renal/electrolyte safety, anemia/infection, and myocardial injury.
tests.management_change: BNP <100 pg/mL or NT-proBNP <300 pg/mL in acute dyspnea makes acute HF less likely; non-acute rule-out thresholds are lower (BNP <35 pg/mL, NT-proBNP <125 pg/mL). Elevated values support HF in context but are affected by age, atrial fibrillation, kidney disease, and obesity; abnormal ECG/CXR/labs change diuresis, oxygen, antibiotics, ischemia evaluation, echo, and disposition.

tests.label: Diuresis and respiratory-support safety review
tests.reason: When dyspnea is treated as HF/volume overload, bedside exam should be paired with renal, electrolyte, oxygenation, and trajectory data so diuresis and respiratory support are safe.
tests.diagnostic_target: Treatment-safety data: oxygen trajectory, renal function, potassium/magnesium/sodium abnormalities, acid-base or ventilatory failure, and response to diuresis or respiratory support.
tests.management_change: Worsening oxygen need, rising creatinine, severe electrolyte abnormality, hypercapnia/acidosis, poor urine output, or poor diuretic response changes monitoring, diuretic dose/route, potassium/magnesium replacement, ventilatory support, and escalation.

red_flags.label: Respiratory failure escalation cues
red_flags.reason: Dyspnea workups must explicitly screen for respiratory failure rather than presenting lung findings as routine descriptive exam data.
red_flags.diagnostic_target: Impending or established respiratory failure: severe hypoxemia, escalating oxygen, fatigue, cyanosis, altered mentation, hypercapnia, or acidemia.
red_flags.management_change: These cues change urgency of respiratory therapy, noninvasive/invasive ventilatory support, ED/ICU escalation, ABG/VBG review, and close reassessment.

red_flags.label: Shock or acute heart-failure escalation cues
red_flags.reason: Possible acute HF can become a perfusion or hypertensive pulmonary-edema emergency; those findings should be explicit and management-linked.
red_flags.diagnostic_target: High-risk cardiac dyspnea clue: shock, poor perfusion, oliguria, ischemia, dangerous arrhythmia, syncope, flash pulmonary edema, hypertensive emergency, or acute valvular complication.
red_flags.management_change: These cues change monitoring location, IV therapy urgency, ECG/troponin/echo priority, vasodilator/diuretic/inotrope discussion, cardiology/critical-care involvement, and ED/ICU disposition.

evidence_metadata.label: Dyspnea/HF initial tests and reference thresholds
evidence_metadata.reason: HF/dyspnea etiology and safety screen: congestion/cardiomegaly or alternative disease on CXR, rhythm/ischemia on ECG, natriuretic peptide support or exclusion of HF, renal/electrolyte safety, anemia/infection, and myocardial injury.
evidence_metadata.diagnostic_target: HF/dyspnea etiology and safety screen: congestion/cardiomegaly or alternative disease on CXR, rhythm/ischemia on ECG, natriuretic peptide support or exclusion of HF, renal/electrolyte safety, anemia/infection, and myocardial injury.

evidence_metadata.label: Diuresis and respiratory-support safety review
evidence_metadata.reason: Treatment-safety data: oxygen trajectory, renal function, potassium/magnesium/sodium abnormalities, acid-base or ventilatory failure, and response to diuresis or respiratory support.
evidence_metadata.diagnostic_target: Treatment-safety data: oxygen trajectory, renal function, potassium/magnesium/sodium abnormalities, acid-base or ventilatory failure, and response to diuresis or respiratory support.

evidence_metadata.label: Respiratory failure escalation cues
evidence_metadata.reason: Impending or established respiratory failure: severe hypoxemia, escalating oxygen, fatigue, cyanosis, altered mentation, hypercapnia, or acidemia.
evidence_metadata.diagnostic_target: Impending or established respiratory failure: severe hypoxemia, escalating oxygen, fatigue, cyanosis, altered mentation, hypercapnia, or acidemia.

evidence_metadata.label: Shock or acute heart-failure escalation cues
evidence_metadata.reason: High-risk cardiac dyspnea clue: shock, poor perfusion, oliguria, ischemia, dangerous arrhythmia, syncope, flash pulmonary edema, hypertensive emergency, or acute valvular complication.
evidence_metadata.diagnostic_target: High-risk cardiac dyspnea clue: shock, poor perfusion, oliguria, ischemia, dangerous arrhythmia, syncope, flash pulmonary edema, hypertensive emergency, or acute valvular complication.

evidence_metadata.label: What changed with breathing: onset, exertional dyspnea, orthopnea or PND, cough/wheeze, chest pain, leg swelling, weight change, oxygen need, or missed diuretics?
evidence_metadata.reason: Dyspnea/heart-failure source and severity: pulmonary congestion, obstructive/infectious symptoms, ischemic symptoms, oxygen escalation, or volume change.
evidence_metadata.diagnostic_target: Dyspnea/heart-failure source and severity: pulmonary congestion, obstructive/infectious symptoms, ischemic symptoms, oxygen escalation, or volume change.

evidence_metadata.label: Blood pressure
evidence_metadata.reason: Basic bedside safety: hypotension, hypertension, shock physiology, pain response, medication effect, or disposition-changing instability.
evidence_metadata.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: hypotension, hypertension, shock physiology, pain response, medication effect, or disposition-changing instability.

evidence_metadata.label: Heart rate
evidence_metadata.reason: Basic bedside safety: tachycardia, bradycardia, irregular rhythm, shock physiology, adrenergic state, infection, pain, or medication effect.
evidence_metadata.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: tachycardia, bradycardia, irregular rhythm, shock physiology, adrenergic state, infection, pain, or medication effect.

evidence_metadata.label: Respiratory rate
evidence_metadata.reason: Basic bedside safety: respiratory distress, compensation, shock physiology, acid-base stress, sedation, or cardiopulmonary deterioration.
evidence_metadata.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: respiratory distress, compensation, shock physiology, acid-base stress, sedation, or cardiopulmonary deterioration.

evidence_metadata.label: Oxygen saturation / support
evidence_metadata.reason: Basic bedside safety: hypoxemia, escalating oxygen need, respiratory failure risk, shock physiology, pulmonary disease, or cardiopulmonary deterioration.
evidence_metadata.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: hypoxemia, escalating oxygen need, respiratory failure risk, shock physiology, pulmonary disease, or cardiopulmonary deterioration.

evidence_metadata.label: Temperature
evidence_metadata.reason: Basic bedside safety: fever, hypothermia, infection, drug reaction, endocrine crisis, inflammatory disease, or systemic illness.
evidence_metadata.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: fever, hypothermia, infection, drug reaction, endocrine crisis, inflammatory disease, or systemic illness.

evidence_metadata.label: PMI
evidence_metadata.reason: Precordial structural clue: displaced/diffuse apical impulse or visible heave in cardiomyopathy/volume-overload context.
evidence_metadata.diagnostic_target: Precordial structural clue: displaced/diffuse apical impulse or visible heave in cardiomyopathy/volume-overload context.
evidence_metadata.evidence: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac exam resources

top_retrieved.label: Respiratory rate
top_retrieved.management_change: Abnormal vitals or perfusion findings can trigger urgent reassessment, resuscitation, sepsis/shock pathway review, or escalation.
top_retrieved.evidence: Surviving Sepsis Campaign guideline; vital-sign trend interpreted in clinical context

top_retrieved.label: Posterior lung sounds
top_retrieved.diagnostic_target: focal consolidation, effusion, wheeze, work of breathing, or ventilation abnormality
top_retrieved.management_change: New focal pulmonary findings can prompt oxygen/support reassessment, imaging review, antibiotics/bronchodilator consideration, or escalation.

top_retrieved.label: Lateral lung sounds
top_retrieved.diagnostic_target: focal consolidation, effusion, wheeze, work of breathing, or ventilation abnormality
top_retrieved.management_change: New focal pulmonary findings can prompt oxygen/support reassessment, imaging review, antibiotics/bronchodilator consideration, or escalation.

top_retrieved.label: JVP
top_retrieved.diagnostic_target: elevated right-sided filling pressure or venous congestion
top_retrieved.management_change: Abnormal congestion findings can support diuresis decisions, escalation of respiratory support assessment, daily volume trending, or need for cardiac evaluation in context.
top_retrieved.evidence: Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension

top_retrieved.label: Lower extremity edema
top_retrieved.diagnostic_target: peripheral edema pattern and volume overload clue
top_retrieved.management_change: Abnormal congestion findings can support diuresis decisions, escalation of respiratory support assessment, daily volume trending, or need for cardiac evaluation in context.
top_retrieved.evidence: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources

top_retrieved.label: Heart sounds
top_retrieved.diagnostic_target: focal consolidation, effusion, wheeze, work of breathing, or ventilation abnormality
top_retrieved.management_change: New focal pulmonary findings can prompt oxygen/support reassessment, imaging review, antibiotics/bronchodilator consideration, or escalation.
top_retrieved.evidence: JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds

review_notes.detail: Respiratory rate (183): Replaced by the validated respiratory rate safety-floor item so basic bedside data stays separate from physical exam maneuvers.
Lateral lung sounds (170): Differentiates congestion, wheeze, consolidation, effusion, or other pulmonary causes.
Blood pressure (138): Replaced by the validated blood pressure safety-floor item so basic bedside data stays separate from physical exam maneuvers.
Posterior tibial pulses (137): Not promoted because the selected validated clinical intent did not define this as a core or conditional bedside item.
Dorsalis pedis pulses (137): Not promoted because the selected validated clinical intent did not define this as a core or conditional bedside item.
Anterior lung sounds (126): Differentiates congestion, wheeze, consolidation, effusion, or other pulmonary causes.
Tactile fremitus (125): Not promoted because the selected validated clinical intent did not define this as a core or conditional bedside item.
Anterior lung percussion (125): Not promoted because the selected validated clinical intent did not define this as a core or conditional bedside item.
